senses.gloss: The meaning of this term is uncertain. Possibilities include

examples.text: كَلَّا إِنَّ كِتَابَ الْأَبْرَارِ لَفِي عِلِّيِّينَ وَمَا أَدْرَاكَ مَا عِلِّيُّونَ
examples.english: Forsooth the record of the righteous is in ʿilliyyīn, and what makes you know what ʿilliyyūn is?

senses.gloss: The meaning of this term is uncertain. Possibilities include:; God, Allah

The meaning of this term is uncertain. Possibilities include:; the heaven, Heaven, the empyrean, or the welkin, a place that’s only accessible to divines. It’s usually depicted higher than the seven heavens and the Heavenly Chamber.
The meaning of this term is uncertain. Possibilities include:; the paradise (garden group), Paradise, Elysium, or the Garden of Eden.
The meaning of this term is uncertain. Possibilities include:; the paradise (garden group), Paradise, Elysium, or the Garden of Eden.; the uppermost paradise of the celestial garden.
The meaning of this term is uncertain. Possibilities include:; a list of deeds of mortals kept in such a place
The meaning of this term is uncertain. Possibilities include:; godly wardens who keep a list of deeds of mortals or other special knowledge
The meaning of this term is uncertain. Possibilities include:; the seventh heaven; Araboth.

etymology.text:
عَلِيّ (ʕaliyy) + ـُونَ (-ūna)

عِلِّيّ (ʕilliyy) + ـُونَ (-ūna)

From a merger of Hebrew עֶלְיוֹן (ʿelyṓn, “high, upper; God”) and the root ع ل ي (ʕ l y), with the last segment of the Hebrew word being taken to be a masculine plural suffix appended to عِلِّيّ (ʕilliyy), hence blending with the pluralizing inflection عِلِّيّ (ʕilliyy) + ـُونَ (-ūna).
